nodes.context: Chinese American street gang in New York City's Chinatown, active from 1967 to 1994, involved in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, etc.
Chinese organized crime group, affiliated with the Flying Dragons.
Chinese American street gang, rival of the Flying Dragons during the 1980s.
Italian-American organized crime group that lost heroin trade due to Pizza Connection prosecutions in mid-1980s.
Former branch of the Flying Dragons consisting of primarily Vietnamese members, existed until at least 1987.
Vietnamese gang formed by David Thai, rival of the Flying Dragons and Ghost Shadows.

edges.evidence_quote: they were affiliated with the Hip Sing Tong
edges.context: The Flying Dragons were affiliated with the Hip Sing Tong.

edges.evidence_quote: During the 1980s, the gang often engaged in bloody turf wars with the newer Ghost Shadows gang.
edges.context: The Flying Dragons fought turf wars with the Ghost Shadows in the 1980s.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang moved heavily into heroin trafficking after the Italian-American Mafia lost the trade as a result of the Pizza Connection prosecutions in the mid-1980s.
edges.context: The Flying Dragons moved into heroin trafficking after the Italian-American Mafia lost the trade.

edges.evidence_quote: The Vietnamese Flying Dragons were a former branch of the Flying Dragons gang that consisted of primarily Vietnamese members.
edges.context: The Vietnamese Flying Dragons were a former branch of the Flying Dragons.

edges.evidence_quote: David Thai later built his own gang that rivalled the Flying Dragons, called Born to Kill
edges.context: Born to Kill rivalled the Flying Dragons.
The Flying Dragons had the Hip Sing Association as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Born to Kill , Ghost Shadows
edges.context: The Flying Dragons had the Ghost Shadows as a rival.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Born to Kill , Ghost Shadows
edges.context: The Flying Dragons had Born to Kill as a rival.
